svg.text: The Great Gatsby
Chapter 3 • Page 47 of 180
The lawn started at the beach and ran toward
the front door for a quarter-mile, jumping over
sun-dials and brick walls and burning gardens—
finally when it reached the house drifting up the
side in bright vines as though from the momentum
of its run. The front was broken by a line of French
windows, glowing now with reflected gold and
wide open to the warm windy afternoon, and Tom
and Daisy lay upon an enormous couch, like
silver idols weighing down that side of the divan
and towers.
Their house was even more elaborate than I had
expected, a cheerful red-and-white Georgian
Colonial mansion overlooking the bay. The lawn
started at the beach and ran toward the front door
for a quarter-mile, jumping over sun-dials and
brick walls and burning gardens—finally when it
reached the house drifting up the side in bright
vines as though from the momentum of its run.
The front was broken by a line of French windows
glowing with reflected gold and wide open to the
warm windy afternoon.
